changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Steadily Turning"

TextENGNew: "...Oh, Akira-chan. Do you need something from our class?"
"Ah, no. Not for you, Tama — for Chisa."
"For me~? Oh my, what a pickle. Ah, but I've got a lot going on this week — is next week okay?"
"...What are you talking about?"
"What, you're asking me on a date, right? Oh, and for dinner I'd love a full-course French meal, by the way~."
"No I'm not!! I just came to get the textbook I lent you back!!"
"Come on, it was just a little joke. Don't get so worked up about it."
Still grumbling, I dug the textbook out from inside my desk and shoved it at him with a casual "here."
"...? What's the matter, Akira?"
"Oh, no, it's nothing..."
Akira was looking around the classroom suspiciously as he took the textbook. It was so obvious what he was doing that I couldn't help breaking into a wicked grin.
changeReason: Original says 'carefully surveyed,' but the Japanese 訝しげに means 'suspiciously/questioningly,' which reveals Akira's transparent ulterior motive (looking for Natsumi) rather than suggesting a neutral, careful observation.

TextENGNew: "If it's Natsumi you're looking for, she's in the bathroom. Want me to sneak you in for a peek~?"
"—?! I-I don't have any such interest!"
"Ahahahaha! So that really was why you came~!"
"Ugh...! W-well, see you!"
Akira went red all the way to his ears and couldn't find a single word to say. He spun on his heel and fled the classroom at a half-trot.
"Hehehe...! Honestly, Akira is hopeless~. He doesn't need an excuse to come see Natsumi, he could just show up."
"Akira-chan is pure-hearted. Unlike a certain someone."
"'A certain someone'? And who would that be?"
"Hmm~? I wonder who~?"
"Hey~!"
"Ahahahaha! ...And besides, I think it just shows how genuine his feelings for Natsumi are... you know?"
"I was... pretty serious about it too, you know?"
"I know. I do understand, in my own way. That's why, to be honest... I'm still not sure what I should do."
"Then... if I said I wanted to ruin it, would you help me?"
"Hmm. If that's what you really want, I'd lend a hand just the once. ...Are you serious?"
It would be easy to crush her feelings. Two or three words would be enough... and she would seal away those feelings forever.
I probably understand her personality better than Akira does. If I decided to put my own feelings first, there were any number of ways I could go about it.
In an entirely fair, and entirely effective, way——.
"Chisato..."
"...Oh come on, Tama-san. What kind of face is that?"
I gave Tamako's bangs a little flick, then let out a slow breath and stretched my arms wide above my head.
"Well, speaking honestly — they obviously like each other, so my real feeling is 'just get on with it already, both of you!' Teasing them is fun and all, but it gets old if that's all it ever is~."
changeReason: Original omits the quoted exclamation 『二人ともしっかりせんかいっ！』('Pull yourselves together, both of you!'), which is the tip's title line and Chisato's defining sentiment; replacing it with a generic 'wish them the best' loses the specific frustrated-yet-caring tone.

TextENGNew: "Ahahaha, really. The way they are, if they ever went on a date they'd both be a bundle of nerves the whole time and wear themselves out."
"Hmm... all right. Maybe we should roll up our sleeves and give them a hand!"
"'Roll up our sleeves'... coming from you, Chisato, that sounds somehow indecent..."
"How rude! I'm thinking of a very fair and effective approach..."
Maybe one day... I'll come to regret this enormously.
But.
Right now, this felt like the best choice I could make.
